study_goal: Use conditional expectation as an F-measurable Radon-Nikodym object and compute it through partitions, densities, independence, and projection.
prelim_angle: Nearly every martingale proof is a conditional-expectation proof in disguise.

study_goal: Build martingales from random walks, stop them, and prove almost sure convergence using upcrossings.
prelim_angle: The core toolkit for fair-game and hitting-time problems.

study_goal: Recognize martingale structures in urns, conditional Borel-Cantelli, likelihood ratios, and branching processes.
prelim_angle: Turns abstract convergence theorems into reusable templates for dependent processes.

section: 4.4 Doob's Inequality, Convergence in Lp, p>1
study_goal: Control martingale maxima and prove Lp convergence using Doob inequalities.
prelim_angle: Use when an exam asks for sup bounds, convergence in Lp, or variance control.

study_goal: Use predictable quadratic variation to prove convergence and strong-law type martingale results.
prelim_angle: A more flexible replacement for independent-increment variance summation.

study_goal: Understand when almost sure martingale limits also converge in L1 and preserve expectations.
prelim_angle: This is the checkpoint before using optional stopping at unbounded times.

study_goal: Apply martingale convergence to decreasing filtrations and exchangeable information.
prelim_angle: Useful for tail, invariant, and exchangeability questions.

study_goal: Know the hypotheses that make optional stopping valid and apply them to random walks.
prelim_angle: One of the highest-yield prelim sections for exact hitting probabilities and expected times.

study_goal: Count simple-random-walk paths using reflection, ballot formulas, and arcsine asymptotics.
prelim_angle: Provides exact combinatorial alternatives to martingale random-walk arguments.
